content: We can reframe the vanilla Self-Refine framework as a single-perspective baseline that can be augmented by integrating multiple inconsistent self-reflection perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanism research, creating a multi-perspective Self-Refine that outperforms the original framework without adding any external data or training overhead. This hybrid approach addresses the core limitation of original Self-Refine: its tendency to get stuck in local optimization loops by repeating the same uncorrected flaws in its single perspective of its own output.
